action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed immediately on processor call with IndexError (image_grid_thw index 1 out of bounds for size 1) after loading data and printing trainable params (r=16 this time); visual merger mismatch on load (reinit OK). predict.py complete and ready. test_prep confirms ~7k samples with bbox/point in abs pixels. Analysis: Manual vision_string + separate image_processor call + full_text with embedded pads leads to internal processor bug on grid indexing (likely counts wrong #images or shape (1,3) mishandled when pads pre-inserted in text). Standard fix per all prior analyses: switch to messages list + processor.apply_chat_template (prompt with add_generation_prompt=True + full conversation) for both prompt_len calc and full inputs; this guarantees consistent placeholder count, grid, and feature/token alignment in one coherent codepath without manual num_tokens math. Use compact non-truncated code, reduced LoRA (language modules only, r=8), lr=1e-4, 32 samples/16 steps for fast ~20s iter + metrics logging. No manual resize or dual mismatched calls. This matches Qwen2.5-VL SFT pattern for grounding and should produce first successful checkpoint. Decision: Overwrite train.py with fixed messages-based implementation, run test_prep+train to validate, then submit_eval on the resulting checkpoint for first grounding_score + time measurement before scaling samples/epochs/LR.
